hero_subtitle: SFTY helps compliance teams consolidate fragmented provider outputs into one structured workflow for onboarding, monitoring, case management, and audit-ready reviews.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance slows when providers stay disconnected
problem_description: Digital asset teams face operational delays and duplicated effort when KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule, and screening outputs remain siloed across multiple provider systems. This fragmentation weakens audit readiness and obscures the full compliance picture needed for reliable decision making and regulatory review.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule, and screening data into a unified compliance workflow that centralizes case management, decision logs, and audit-ready evidence for faster review and clearer operational control.
workflow_title: From fragmented checks to unified decisions
workflow_description: SFTY aggregates inputs from multiple providers, applies internal rules, routes reviews, records decisions and preserves evidence for consistent compliance control.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another provider. The compliance control layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented provider outputs into one operational workflow that supports decisions, audit evidence, case management and regulatory reporting for digital asset compliance.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with enterprise-grade orchestration, audit-ready evidence and governance to streamline multi-provider compliance and bank due diligence workflows securely.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support connected control across multiple providers
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how SFTY manages workflows across provider outputs
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence is preserved in case reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY connects provider orchestration and compliance rules
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides VASP compliance teams with a connected control layer that connects KYC, KYT, Travel Rule, screening and reporting into one structured compliance workflow.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY connect multiple provider outputs?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates and normalizes data from multiple providers to create linked cases, decision logs and regulatory updates within a single operational workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs, provider outputs and evidence packages to support audit readiness and defensible compliance review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY complements existing tools by connecting their outputs without replacement, supporting operational governance, access control and role-based permissions.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC, KYT or Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but turns their outputs into structured workflows, helping reduce manual fragmentation across compliance operations.
final_title: Turn fragmented providers into one control layer
final_description: Review how SFTY structures multi-provider outputs into connected onboarding, monitoring, case management and audit-ready compliance workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
